content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be augmented by adapting the Corrective RAG (CRAG) framework to grade and correct the relevance of stored episodic memory traces during online curiosity-driven exploration, rather than only correcting retrieval from static external knowledge corpora. This adaptation lets active agents avoid repeating uninformative exploratory actions caused by stale or misclassified episodic memory, improving sample efficiency in sparse-reward environments.
